threshold_count: 5 or more symptoms (must include depressed mood or loss of interest)

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences a markedly diminished interest or pleasure in all, or almost all, activities most of the day.
The patient experiences significant weight loss or gain, or a decrease or increase in appetite nearly every day.
The patient experiences insomnia or hypersomnia nearly every day.
The patient exhibits psychomotor agitation or retardation that is observable by others.
The patient experiences fatigue or a loss of energy nearly every day.
The patient experiences feelings of worthlessness or excessive, inappropriate guilt nearly every day.
The patient experiences a diminished ability to think or concentrate, or indecisiveness, nearly every day.
The patient has recurrent thoughts of death, recurrent suicidal ideation, or makes a suicide attempt.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: At least 2 of 6 symptoms while depressed; never without symptoms for 2+ months

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient has never been without depressive symptoms for more than 2 months at a time during the 2-year period of the disturbance.
The patient experiences poor appetite or overeating while feeling depressed.
The patient experiences insomnia or hypersomnia nearly every day.
The patient experiences fatigue or a loss of energy nearly every day.
The patient suffers from low self-esteem while experiencing depression.
The patient experiences a diminished ability to think or concentrate, or indecisiveness, nearly every day.
The patient experiences feelings of hopelessness while depressed.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Severe tantrums 3 or more times per week; persistently irritable mood between outbursts
duration_rule: 12 months or more (never absent for 3+ months)

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits severe, recurrent temper outbursts that are out of proportion in intensity or duration to the situation or provocation.
The patient exhibits a mood that is persistently irritable or angry for most of the day, nearly every day, between temper outbursts.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 5 or more symptoms (at least 1 must be core mood symptom)
duration_rule: Most menstrual cycles in the past year

symptoms.description: The patient experiences marked affective lability, such as pronounced mood swings or sudden tearfulness, before menstruation.
The patient exhibits marked anger, irritability, or increased interpersonal conflicts during the premenstrual phase.
The patient experiences a markedly depressed mood, feelings of hopelessness, or self-deprecating thoughts before menstruation.
The patient experiences marked anxiety, tension, or feelings of being keyed up or on edge before menstruation.
The patient experiences a decreased interest in usual activities before menstruation.
The patient experiences subjective difficulty in concentrating during the premenstrual phase.
The patient feels lethargic, easily fatigued, or has a marked lack of energy during the premenstrual phase.
The patient experiences a significant change in appetite, overeating, or specific food cravings during the premenstrual phase.
The patient experiences hypersomnia or insomnia during the premenstrual phase.
The patient has a subjective sense of being overwhelmed or out of control during the premenstrual phase.
The patient experiences physical symptoms such as breast tenderness, muscle or joint pain, a sensation of bloating, or weight gain before menstruation.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Depressive Disorder Due to Another Medical Condition
threshold_count: Prominent and enduring depressed mood or loss of interest

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences a markedly diminished interest or pleasure in all, or almost all, activities most of the day.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Prominent and enduring depressed mood or loss of interest
duration_rule: During or soon after intoxication or withdrawal

symptoms.description: The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences a markedly diminished interest or pleasure in all, or almost all, activities most of the day.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: At least one manic episode (3 or more symptoms; 4 if only irritable)

symptoms.description: The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient exhibits a persistent, abnormal increase in goal-directed activity or energy.
The patient experiences inflated self-esteem or grandiosity.
The patient experiences a decreased need for sleep, feeling rested after only a few hours of sleep.
The patient is more talkative than usual or feels a persistent pressure to keep talking.
The patient experiences a flight of ideas or a subjective feeling that their thoughts are racing.
The patient is easily distracted by unimportant or irrelevant external stimuli.
The patient shows a marked increase in goal-directed activity or exhibits psychomotor agitation.
The patient excessively engages in activities that have a high potential for painful consequences, such as buying sprees, sexual indiscretions, or foolish business investments.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: At least 1 hypomanic episode AND at least 1 major depressive episode

symptoms.description: The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient exhibits a persistent, abnormal increase in goal-directed activity or energy.
The patient experiences inflated self-esteem or grandiosity.
The patient experiences a decreased need for sleep, feeling rested after only a few hours of sleep.
The patient is more talkative than usual or feels a persistent pressure to keep talking.
The patient experiences a flight of ideas or a subjective feeling that their thoughts are racing.
The patient is easily distracted by unimportant or irrelevant external stimuli.
The patient shows a marked increase in goal-directed activity or exhibits psychomotor agitation.
The patient excessively engages in activities that have a high potential for painful consequences, such as buying sprees, sexual indiscretions, or foolish business investments.
The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences a markedly diminished interest or pleasure in all, or almost all, activities most of the day.
The patient experiences significant weight loss or gain, or a decrease or increase in appetite nearly every day.
The patient experiences insomnia or hypersomnia nearly every day.
The patient exhibits psychomotor agitation or retardation that is observable by others.
The patient experiences fatigue or a loss of energy nearly every day.
The patient experiences feelings of worthlessness or excessive, inappropriate guilt nearly every day.
The patient experiences a diminished ability to think or concentrate, or indecisiveness, nearly every day.
The patient has recurrent thoughts of death, recurrent suicidal ideation, or makes a suicide attempt.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Numerous periods of hypomanic and depressive symptoms below full episode threshold
duration_rule: 2 years (1 year for children/adolescents); present at least half the time

symptoms.description: The patient has never met the full criteria for a major depressive, manic, or hypomanic episode.
The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient experiences a depressed mood for most of the day, nearly every day.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Bipolar Disorder Due to Another Medical Condition
threshold_count: Physiological cause of manic or hypomanic symptoms

symptoms.description: The patient has evidence from their history, physical examination, or laboratory findings that the disturbance is the direct pathophysiological consequence of another medical condition.
The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient exhibits a persistent, abnormal increase in goal-directed activity or energy.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Manic or hypomanic symptoms following substance use
duration_rule: During or soon after intoxication or withdrawal

symptoms.description: The patient has evidence from their history, physical examination, or laboratory findings that the symptoms developed during or soon after substance intoxication or withdrawal, or after exposure to a medication capable of producing manic or hypomanic symptoms.
The patient experiences an abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood.
The patient exhibits a persistent, abnormal increase in goal-directed activity or energy.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Clinically important symptoms that fall below threshold for defined disorders
duration_rule: Variable (e.g., recurrent brief 2–13 days; short-duration 4–13 days)

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits clinically important depressive symptoms that fall below the diagnostic threshold.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

diagnosis_name: Other Specified Bipolar and Related Disorder
threshold_count: Clinically important symptoms that fall below threshold for defined disorders

symptoms.description: The patient experiences clinically important bipolar symptoms that fall below the diagnostic threshold.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Mood symptoms present but insufficient information for specific diagnosis

symptoms.description: The patient presents with mood symptoms, but there is insufficient information for a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.
